title: Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story
summary: Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explained.

title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
summary: Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them.

title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
summary: Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public.

title: Segmentation Works for OT If Operators Are Paying Attention
summary: Even the best segmentation strategy will fall apart without constant oversight and disciplined operations.

title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
summary: North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the nation's state-sponsored cybercrime groups, which target financial firms and other businesses.

title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
summary: The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred.

title: Bug Bounty Research Triggers ServiceNow Security Alert
summary: Security research inadvertently led organizations to believe they were being breached through their ServiceNow instances.

title: AI Risk Worries Insurers & Businesses Alike
summary: As companies adopt AI, many insurance firms are explicitly excluding AI risks, while others are forging ahead to create the right framework. What risks can firms reasonably manage?

title: Nightmare-Eclipse Drops Yet Another Microsoft Exploit, RoguePlanet
summary: The disgruntled researcher released yet another PoC for a Windows Defender bug that allows for system takeover, showing no signs of abandoning their ongoing feud with Microsoft.

title: The Invisible Battlefield: How Cyberwar Is Reshaping Everyday Life
summary: Former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis warns that cyberattacks threaten hospitals, utilities, and essential services.

title: Blame AI: Patch Tuesday Hits Record 206 CVEs
summary: Voluminous patch updates could soon be the norm, as artificial intelligence accelerates the speed and scale of vulnerability discovery.

title: Microsoft Exchange Flaw Lets Attackers Spoof Any Email Address
summary: “Ghost-Sender" is the result of a widespread misconfiguration, according to researchers, and evidence indicates it's being actively abused in the wild.

title: Miasma Supply Chain Worm Burrows Into 73 Microsoft Repositories
summary: The attacks stemmed from a GitHub account that was also compromised in a previous Miasma attack on Microsoft last month.

title: Russian Attackers Weaponize WinRAR Flaw Against Ukrainian Orgs
summary: Two separate campaigns target CVE-2025-8088, fixed last July, to conduct data theft and cyberespionage against military and government targets in Ukraine.

title: AI Slop Will Kill Cybersecurity Storytelling If We Let It
summary: AI-generated content threatens credibility in cybersecurity. This "Ask the Expert" column explores why human oversight matters and how to maintain authentic narratives.

title: Silent Ransom Group Hits US Law Firms in Escalating Extortion Attacks
summary: The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.

title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
summary: A newly discovered, critical zero-day vulnerability is under attack; a Qilin ransomware affiliate has been blamed for at least one incident.

title: Iran Signed a Ceasefire — Its Hackers Didn't
summary: An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict.

title: 'Hades' Campaign Against PyPI Puts New Spin on Shai-Hulud
summary: The latest attacks, which hit 37 PyPI wheels and 19 code packages, show a continued evolution of the persistent software supply chain threat.

title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
summary: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.

title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
summary: AI worms, or "viruses with wings and brains," adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say.

title: Trump AI Order Seeks Voluntary Frontier Model Testing
summary: The White House's executive order establishes voluntary framework for early government access to frontier models while investing in federal security.

title: Rust-Written IronWorm Hits NPM Supply Chain
summary: Like Shai-Hulud, the campaign targets developers to steal credentials and reuses them to propagate across the software supply channel.

title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
summary: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.

title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
summary: Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections.

title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
summary: Organizations are growing serious about which nation's rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.

title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
summary: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity.

title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
summary: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.

title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
summary: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.

title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
summary: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.

title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
summary: A disabled security setting meant to protect authentication across Android versions of key apps like Word, PowerPoint, and Excel paved the way for attackers to steal logins and data.

title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
summary: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.

title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
summary: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools.

title: Zoom CISO: AI as a Security Enabler, Not Role-Replacer
summary: Zoom CISO Sandra McLeod discusses the challenges of securing a global communication platform, the promise of AI-driven security workflows, and her advice for aspiring cybersecurity leaders.

title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
summary: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.

title: DriveSurge Hijacks Thousands of Sites for ClickFix, FakeUpdate Attacks
summary: A sneaky, wide-scale IAB operation uses a malicious traffic distribution system (TDS) to redirect visitors of trusted websites to ones that deliver malware.

title: China Uses Dual-Method Cyberattack on Czech Orgs
summary: China is stealing data from high-value targets via a sneaky, double-layer spear-phishing campaign that includes the Azureveil malware.

title: Securing AI Agents Before They Go Rogue Is Next to Impossible
summary: High-autonomy agents with broad permissions and unfettered access are a recipe for disaster, and enterprises need to act now before they become the next horror story.

title: Beyond Assume-Breach: How AI-Native Security Will Reshape Enterprise Defense
summary: Twenty years after Dark Reading launched, we're looking ahead at what's next for enterprise security. Spoiler: It's hyper-segmented, AI-orchestrated, and way more sophisticated than your dad's firewall.

title: Anthropic to Open Mythos AI to EU's ENISA
summary: The European security agency's entry to Project Glasswing is the result of "strong bilateral cooperation" between the European Commission and Anthropic.

title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
summary: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.

title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
summary: Exploiting the PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability requires certain conditions, but adversaries have done so in two attack waves that started in mid-May.

title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Cybersecurity) Progress
summary: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary package, we asked readers for a cybersecurity-related caption that captures their thoughts about the industry's last two decades.

title: As Global Powers Explore Humanoid Robots, Cyber-Risk Looms
summary: The future of cybersecurity is germinating, as nation-states vie for dominance in the embodied AI market and its supply chain.

title: Asia's Cyber Insurance Market Shows Signs of Life
summary: The cyber insurance industry has made relatively weak inroads into Asia due to a variety of factors, but that could be changing.

title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
summary: Researchers discover an exploit chain combining over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities that could have compromised a popular automation service.

title: 'The Com' Cyberattacks Support Violence & Sexploitation
summary: Your organization's security failures have consequences for everyone else as well since this criminal gang uses its cyber winnings to support more violent and widespread crimes.

title: Dutch Raid Fails to Dent Russian Bulletproof Host
summary: Dutch law enforcement seized 800 servers and arrested two operators of THE.Hosting but left the hosting provider's core IP address space intact.

title: Name That Toon Contest
Agentic AI Isn't Risky; the Way Orgs Deploy It Is
summary: AI agents aren't black boxes — they're models interacting with software tools. The risk lies in their overlap.
